hero.question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
hero.prob_take: The market is literally flipping a coin on Iran invasion by 2027, and the 3-point pullback in 24 hours tells you that nobody actually believes we're close to boots on the ground right now. Sharp money would wait for either a material regional escalation (think major Israeli strike that demands U.S. response) or a shift toward de-escalation, because 51.5% doesn't price in the massive political friction a war would face in an election year and beyond.

daily_take.headline: Iran Invasion Odds Cooling. Market Still Pricing in Hair-Trigger Scenario.
daily_take.deck: The 51-51 coin flip on U.S. military action before 2027 just dropped 3 points in 24 hours. Conventional wisdom says Trump means business. But the -3 move signals smart money is hedging on messier alternatives: sanctions escalation, proxy conflicts, cyber ops that stop short of boots on the ground. At current levels, you're betting on full-scale invasion, not regional posturing. That's probably too binary.

sidebar.headline: Chavez-DeRemer's 87% exit odds just jumped 6.5 points. Cabinet musical chairs usually punishes early favorites. Fade the consensus.
sidebar.label: Lori Chavez-DeRemer Exit: 87.0%

sidebar.headline: Arizona shot up 2.2 points to 21.6% and now carries more volume than it did yesterday. Tournament odds rarely move on preseason noise unless the sharps see something.
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 Title: 21.6%

sidebar.headline: Anduril plummeted 22 points overnight. A -$22K swing on a $307K market screams informed selling. Someone knows something about IPO timing or funding rounds.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO Before 2027: 26.0%
